plain_language_interpretation: Mixed sentiment-deterioration case: F&G is still high (greed), but it has fallen from the earlier extreme-greed regime and news intensity is high. FiLM lowers a weak bullish chart-only probability.
Sharp fear-transition case: F&G is low and far below its recent level; FinBERT is slightly negative-leaning. FiLM lowers a weak false-Up chart prediction into the correct Down class.

Clean fear and negative-news case: F&G is in extreme fear, the 60-day baseline is also low, and FinBERT negative ratio exceeds positive ratio. FiLM lowers bullish probability and corrects the false-Up prediction.

Borderline fear correction: chart-only P(up) is barely above 0.5, while F&G is extreme fear, F&G momentum is negative, news count is high, and FinBERT is negative-leaning. A small FiLM adjustment is enough to switch to Down.

Over-correction in positive context: true label is Up and chart-only is correct, but F&G is high and FinBERT is positive-leaning. FiLM still lowers P(up) strongly and turns the prediction into a wrong Down.
Over-correction in greed context: true label is Up, but FiLM applies the same downward calibration under high F&G and positive-leaning news, changing a weak correct Up into a wrong Down.
Late-sample positive-regime regression: F&G is extreme greed and FinBERT positive ratio is high. The downward FiLM calibration is too strong and incorrectly suppresses an Up outcome.
Mixed but positive-leaning regression: F&G is high despite deterioration from its earlier level, and FinBERT remains positive-leaning. FiLM lowers P(up) below 0.5 and creates a false Down.
